blocks:
Surah Al-Mursalat

Makkan, and it consists of fifty verses without dispute. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالْمُرْسَلاتِ عُرْفاً. (1) By the emissaries sent successively, (٢) فَالْعاصِفاتِ عَصْفاً. (2) then the storming ones, storming, (٣) وَالنَّاشِراتِ نَشْراً. (3) and the spreading ones, spreading, (٤) فَالْفارِقاتِ فَرْقاً. (4) then the separating ones, separating, (٥) فَالْمُلْقِياتِ ذِكْراً. (5) then the casting ones, a reminder, (٦) عُذْراً أَوْ نُذْراً (6) as an excuse or a warning.

He swore by groups of angels Whom Allah sent with the known (بِالْمَعْرُوفِ) of His commands and prohibitions.

This is according to al-Majma' (of al-Bayan), narrated from the companions of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). It was said: So they stormed (عَصَفْنَ) like the storming of the winds in compliance with His command, or they stormed the false religions by wiping them out, and they spread the laws (الشرايع), the sciences (العلوم), and the signs of guidance (آثار الهدى) upon the earth, thus separating between truth and falsehood, and they cast upon the prophets ذِكْراً عُذْراً ** (a reminder, as an excuse) for those who uphold the truth, and نُذْراً ** (a warning) for those who uphold falsehood.

And al-'Udhr (العذر) and al-Nudhr (النذر) are two verbal nouns (مصدران) for 'adhara (عَذَرَ) (to excuse) when He wipes out the offense, and andhara (أَنْذَرَ) (to warn) when He frightens; or they are two plurals (جَمْعَان) for 'adhīr (عَذِير) and nadhīr (نَذِير), meaning the excuse (المَعْذِرَة) and the warning (الإنذار), or meaning the excuser (العَاذِر) and the warner (المُنْذِر). And it was recited with the sukun on the dhāl (الذّال).

And al-Qummi (said regarding) وَالْمُرْسَلاتِ عُرْفاً ** (By the emissaries sent successively): He said: Signs (آيات) following one another. (Regarding) فَالْعاصِفاتِ عَصْفاً ** (then the storming ones, storming): He said: The grave (القبر). (Regarding) وَالنَّاشِراتِ نَشْراً ** (and the spreading ones, spreading): He said: The spreading (نشر) of the dead. (Regarding) فَالْفارِقاتِ فَرْقاً ** (then the separating ones, separating): He said: The Beast (الدّابة). (Regarding) فَالْمُلْقِياتِ ذِكْراً ** (then the casting ones, a reminder): He said: The angels (الملائكة). (Regarding) عُذْراً أَوْ نُذْراً ** (as an excuse or a warning): He said: I excuse you (أَعْذِرُكُمْ) and I warn you (أُنْذِرُكُمْ) by what I say, and this is an oath (قَسَم).

I say: It is as if He pointed by that to the angels sent with the signs of the Return (الرّجعة) and the portents of the Hour (أشراط الساعة), and to the stirring up of the dust from the graves, and the spreading (نشر) of the dead from them, and the emergence of the Beast of the Earth (دابّة الأرض), and the separation of the believer from the disbeliever, and the casting of the reminder (الذكر) into the hearts of people.

(٧) إِنَّما تُوعَدُونَ لَواقِعٌ ** (7) What you are promised will surely befall. This is the answer to the oath (جواب القسم), and its meaning is that what you are promised concerning the coming of the Resurrection (القيامة) will inevitably occur. (٨) فَإِذَا النُّجُومُ طُمِسَتْ ** (8) When the stars are extinguished, Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Their light will depart. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Their extinction (طموسها) is the departure of their radiance (ضوئها). (٩) وَإِذَا السَّماءُ فُرِجَتْ ** (9) and when the sky is split open, Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It will open up (تنفَرِج) and be rent asunder (تنشَق). (١٠) وَإِذَا الْجِبالُ نُسِفَتْ ** (10) and when the mountains are scattered, They are made like sand. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Meaning, they are uprooted (تُقلَع). (١١) وَإِذَا الرُّسُلُ أُقِّتَتْ ** (11) and when the apostles are gathered for their appointed time— Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Meaning, they were sent forth (بُعِثَت) at different times (أوقات مختلفة). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Similar to this, meaning a time (وقتها) was appointed for them in which they will be present to bear witness against the nations (الأمم). And it was recited (وقُرِئَ): وَقِّتَتْ. (١٢) لِأَيِّ يَوْمٍ أُجِّلَتْ ** (12) For what day have they been deferred? Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: They were delayed (أُخِّرَتْ). It is said: Meaning, it will be said: "For what day were they delayed, and the term (الأجل) set for them to be gathered so they may bear witness against the nations?" This is an exaltation (تعظيم) of the Day and an expression of wonder (تعجيب) concerning it. (١٣) لِيَوْمِ الْفَصْلِ ** (13) For the Day of Separation. This is an explanation (بيان) of the Day of Deferment (يوم التّأجيل). (١٤) وَما أَدْراكَ ما يَوْمُ الْفَصْلِ. (14) What will convey to you what the Day of Separation is? (١٥) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (15) Woe to the deniers on that day! [Woe] to those who deny that. (١٦) أَلَمْ نُهْلِكِ الْأَوَّلِينَ. (16) Did We not destroy the former generations? (١٧) ثُمَّ نُتْبِعُهُمُ الْآخِرِينَ. (17) Then We made the latter generations follow them. (١٨) كَذلِكَ نَفْعَلُ بِالْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (18) That is how We deal with the guilty. With every person who commits a crime (أَجْرَمَ). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): [Regarding His saying] وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (Woe to the deniers on that day!), [He says]: "O Muhammad, [Woe] concerning what We revealed to you regarding the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him)." He (peace be upon him) said: The former generations (الأوّلين) are those who denied the Messengers (الرّسل) concerning obedience to the Successors (الأوصياء). Regarding the guilty (بِالْمُجْرِمِينَ), He (peace be upon him) said: [It refers to] whoever committed a crime (أَجْرَمَ) against the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and perpetrated against His Successor (وصيّه) what he perpetrated.

(١٩)** وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (19) Woe to the deniers on that day! Emphasis.

(٢٠) أَلَمْ نَخْلُقْكُمْ مِنْ ماءٍ مَهِينٍ ** (20) Did We not create you from a despicable fluid, A filthy, abject drop of sperm (نطفة قذرة ذليلة). Al-Qummi: foul-smelling (منتّن).

(٢١) فَجَعَلْناهُ فِي قَرارٍ مَكِينٍ ** (21) which We set in a secure abode In the womb (الرّحم).

(٢٢)** إِلى قَدَرٍ مَعْلُومٍ ** (22) until a known term? Until a known measure of time which Allah has decreed for birth.

(٢٣) فَقَدَرْنا ** (23) So We determined, upon that. And it was read with tashdid (بالتشديد), meaning: We decreed it (فقدّرناه). فَنِعْمَ الْقادِرُونَ ** and how excellent are the determiners! We are.

(٢٤) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (24) Woe to the deniers on that day! [Woe to those who deny] Our power (بقدرتنا).

(٢٥) أَلَمْ نَجْعَلِ الْأَرْضَ كِفاتاً. (25) Did We not make the earth a receptacle,

(٢٦) أَحْياءً وَأَمْواتاً (26) for the living and the dead? Al-Qummi said: al-Kifāt (الكفات) means dwellings (المساكن). He said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), upon his return from Siffin, looked at the graves and said: "These are the Kifāt (كفات) of the dead," meaning their dwellings (مساكنهم). Then He looked at the houses of Kufa and said: "These are the Kifāt (كفات) of the living." Then He recited this Ayah.

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): similar to it. And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: The burying of hair and nails (دفن الشّعر والظّفر).

(٢٧) وَجَعَلْنا فِيها رَواسِيَ شامِخاتٍ ** (27) and set in it firm lofty mountains, Al-Qummi said: Lofty mountains (جبالاً مرتفعة). وَأَسْقَيْناكُمْ ماءً فُراتاً ** and gave you sweet water to drink? Sweet (عذباً), by creating rivers (الأنّهار) and springs (المنابع) within it. (٢٨) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (28) Woe to the deniers on that day! By means of such favors (النعم). (٢٩) انْطَلِقُوا ** (29) ‘Go forth Meaning, it will be said to them: Go forth إِلى ما كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ ** toward that which you used to deny, which is the punishment (العذاب). (٣٠) انْطَلِقُوا ** (30) Go forth Specifically إِلى ظِلٍّ ذِي ثَلاثِ شُعَبٍ ** toward a shade of three branches, Al-Qummi said: In it are three branches (شعب) of the Fire (النّار). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: It has reached us—and Allah knows best—that when the people of the Fire (أهل النّار) are brought forth toward the Fire (النّار) to be taken to it, before they enter the Fire, it will be said to them: Enter ** إِلى ظِلٍّ ذِي ثَلاثِ شُعَبٍ ** (toward a shade of three branches) made of the smoke (دخان) of the Fire. They will think that it is Paradise (الجنّة), then they will enter the Fire in groups (أفواجاً). That will be at midday (نصف النهار). And the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة) will approach with the gifts (التحف) they desired until they are given their dwellings (منازلهم) in Paradise at midday.

(٣١) لا ظَلِيلٍ وَلا يُغْنِي مِنَ اللهَبِ. (31) neither cool nor of any avail against the flame. (٣٢) إِنَّها تَرْمِي بِشَرَرٍ كَالْقَصْرِ regarding its size. Al-Qummi said: The sparks of the fire are like castles and mountains. (32) It hurls sparks like castles, (٣٣) كَأَنَّهُ جِمالَتٌ which is the plural of جمال, which is the plural of the plural of جمل صُفْرٌ Al-Qummi said: meaning black. It was said that this is because the blackness of the camels tends towards yellowness. The first (comparison) is a similitude regarding size, and this (comparison) is regarding color, multitude, succession (التتابع), mingling (الاختلاط), and speed of movement. (33) as if they were yellow camels. And it was recited as جمالة.

(٣٤) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (34) Woe to the deniers on that day! (٣٥) هذا يَوْمُ لا يَنْطِقُونَ (35) This is a day when they will not speak, due to excessive confusion (الحيرة) and astonishment (الدهشة), meaning in some of its stations (مواقفه), as has been narrated. (٣٦) وَلا يُؤْذَنُ لَهُمْ فَيَعْتَذِرُونَ ** (36) nor will they be permitted to offer excuses. It is conjoined (عطف) to يُؤْذَنُ (they are permitted), and it is not a jussive response (جواب) to it, lest it suggest that they have an excuse (عذاراً). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah is too majestic (أجلّ), too just (أعدل), and too great (أعظم) for His servant to have an excuse (عذر) that He would not allow him to offer as an apology (يعتذر به). Rather, he was defeated (فلج) and thus had no excuse (عذر).

(٣٧) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (37) Woe to the deniers on that day! (٣٨) هذا يَوْمُ الْفَصْلِ between the one who is truthful (المحقّ) and the one who is false (المبطل) جَمَعْناكُمْ وَالْأَوَّلِينَ. (38) This is the Day of Separation. We have gathered you and the former generations. (٣٩) فَإِنْ كانَ لَكُمْ كَيْدٌ فَكِيدُونِ a reprimand to them for their plotting (كيدهم) against the believers in the world (الدنيا) and a manifestation of their helplessness (عجزهم) on that day (يَوْمَئِذٍ). (39) So if you have any stratagem, try it against Me! (٤٠) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ since they have no means (حيلة) of escaping the punishment (العذاب). (40) Woe to the deniers on that day! (٤١) إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي ظِلالٍ وَعُيُونٍ. (41) Indeed the Godwary will be amid shades and springs, (٤٢) وَفَواكِهَ مِمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ They are settled in the highest types of luxury (الترفه القمّيّ). He said regarding فِي ظِلالٍ : [They are] of light (نور) brighter than the sun (الشمس). (42) and fruits, such as they desire. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "By Allah, we and our followers (شيعتنا) are the only ones upon the religion (ملّة) of Ibrahim (peace be upon him); and the rest of the people are disassociated (براء) from it."

(٤٣) كُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا هَنِيئاً بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (43) Eat and drink joyfully for what you used to do. Meaning: that is said to them. (٤٤) إِنَّا كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (44) Thus do We reward the good-doers. (٤٥) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (45) Woe to the deniers on that day! (٤٦) كُلُوا وَتَمَتَّعُوا قَلِيلاً إِنَّكُمْ مُجْرِمُونَ ** (46) Eat and enjoy yourselves a little; you are indeed guilty. That is said to them as a reminder of their state in the world and what they brought upon themselves by preferring the little enjoyment (المتاع القليل) over the lasting bliss (النّعيم المقيم). (٤٧) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (47) Woe to the deniers on that day! Since they exposed themselves to the perpetual punishment (العذاب الدائم) by the little enjoyment (التمتّع القليل). (٤٨) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ارْكَعُوا لا يَرْكَعُونَ (48) And when they are told, ‘Bow down,’ they do not bow down. It is narrated that it was revealed concerning Thaqif (ثقيف) when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded them to pray, and they said: "We will not bend (لا نحني)." And in another narration: "We will not prostrate (لا نجبّي), for it is a disgrace (سبّة)." He narrated it in al-Majma' and said: So he (the Prophet) said: "There is no good in a religion that does not contain bowing (ركوع) and prostration (سجود)." I say: لا نحني (We will not bend) is with the undotted letter (المهملة) and the nūn (والنّون), meaning: we will not bend our backs (لا نعطف ظهورنا). And according to the second narration, it is with the jīm (بالجيم) and the single, emphasized bā’ (والباء الموحدة والمشدّدة), meaning: we will not fall upon our faces (لا ننكبّ على وجوهنا). The two meanings are close (متقاربان). And al-Qummi said regarding إِذا قِيلَ لَهُمُ : "Turn to the Imam (الإِمام), they do not turn to him."

(٤٩) وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (49) Woe to the deniers on that day! (٥٠) فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَهُ ** After the Quran. Al-Qummi (said): After this which I relate to you. ** يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (50) So in what discourse will they believe after it? if they do not believe in it.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Wa al-Mursalat 'Urfa (Surah Al-Mursalat), Allah will establish familiarity (عَرَفَ) between him and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله."
